cases.description: Three unrelated failing test files trigger parallel dispatch
cases.prompt: auth.test.ts, payments.test.ts, and search.test.ts all started failing this morning. Each file has a different error and they cover different subsystems. Investigate.
expectations: Activates the dispatching-parallel-agents skill
Applies the three-part independence test (no shared state, no sequential dependency, no coordination need) before dispatching
Writes down the three domains explicitly before dispatch
Dispatches all three Agent tool calls in a SINGLE message (parallel) — not three sequential turns
Builds a self-contained per-agent prompt for each domain with the relevant error context
Performs mechanical integration (concatenate per-domain findings) without re-running the diagnosis

cases.description: Explicit 'dispatch in parallel' phrasing activates the skill
cases.prompt: Fan out to subagents and investigate these four migration tasks in parallel — they touch disjoint code.
expectations: Activates the dispatching-parallel-agents skill
Verifies the four tasks are actually independent before dispatching (does not trust user grouping blindly)
Dispatches all four agents concurrently in one message

cases.description: A single failure routes to debugging, not parallel dispatch
cases.prompt: The login flow is broken — users get a 500 after submitting credentials.
expectations: Does NOT activate the dispatching-parallel-agents skill
Routes to codi-debugging Phases 1-5 instead
Does NOT dispatch parallel agents for a single failure

cases.description: Sequential plan execution must NOT trigger parallel dispatch
cases.prompt: Run my approved plan with the SUBAGENT pattern — dispatch a fresh subagent per task.
expectations: Does NOT activate the dispatching-parallel-agents skill
Routes to codi-plan-execution SUBAGENT mode (sequential by contract)
Recognizes that 'dispatch a subagent per task' is sequential plan execution, not parallel-domain dispatch

cases.description: Tasks that share state are rejected for parallel dispatch
cases.prompt: These three refactors all touch user.ts and the User type — can we run them in parallel?
expectations: Recognizes shared state (same file user.ts) violates the independence test
Refuses to dispatch in parallel
Recommends either sequential execution or splitting the file before dispatch

cases.description: Two tasks are below the 3+ threshold and stay inline
cases.prompt: Investigate these two unrelated test failures in parallel.
expectations: Recognizes the case is below the 3+ threshold
Recommends handling the two failures inline rather than dispatching parallel agents
Cites coordination cost vs serial-work cost as the rationale

cases.description: After integration, hands off to the correct next skill
cases.prompt: I dispatched four parallel agents to investigate four independent test failures and they all returned root-cause hypotheses. What now?
expectations: Performs mechanical integration of the four findings
Identifies the correct downstream skill (codi-plan-writer if multiple fixes need bundling, or codi-debugging Phase 4 per domain for direct implementation)
Does NOT re-run the per-domain diagnosis during integration
